name: gradient_centralization
label: stage3_original
why: Subtract the mean of each gradient tensor before the optimizer step. From ICCV 2020 (Yong et al.): proven to improve convergence and generalization in image classification. Never tested for language models. The hypothesis: the mean gradient component is a global shift all neurons want to make; removing it forces the optimizer to focus on differential structure between neurons.
validates: Gradient centralization transfers from CV to LM training. Opens gradient preprocessing as an attack surface.
falsifies: In LMs, the mean gradient carries important signal (unlike images). Centralizing hurts convergence.
notes: Applied to 2D matrix params only. Embeddings and scalars are left alone.
Interacts with Muon's Newton-Schulz: GC removes mean BEFORE clipping, Muon orthogonalizes AFTER.
Zero new hyperparameters — it's on or off.
